kairos 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

Chronological Apportioning

Source: R/AllGenerics.R, R/apportion.R
apportion.Rd

Chronological Apportioning

apportion(object, ...)

# S4 method for CountMatrix
apportion(
  object,
  s0,
  s1,
  t0,
  t1,
  from = min(s0),
  to = max(s1),
  step = 25,
  method = c("uniform", "truncated"),
  z = 2
)

Arguments

object

An \(m \times p\) arkhe::CountMatrix object.
...

Currently not used.
s0

A length-\(m\) numeric vector giving the site beginning dates (in years AD).
s1

A length-\(m\) numeric vector giving the site end dates (in years AD).
t0

A length-\(p\) numeric vector giving the type end dates (in years AD).
t1

A length-\(p\) numeric vector giving the type end dates (in years AD).
from

A length-one numeric vector.
to

A length-one numeric vector.
step

A length-one integer vector giving the step size, i.e. the width of each time step for apportioning (in years AD; defaults to \(25\)).
method

A character string specifying the distribution to be used. It must be one of "uniform" (uniform distribution) or "truncated" (truncated standard normal distribution). Any unambiguous substring can be given.
z

An integer value giving the lower and upper truncation points (defaults to \(2\)).

References

Roberts, J. M., Mills, B. J., Clark, J. J., Haas, W. R., Huntley, D. L. & Trowbridge, M. A. (2012). A Method for Chronological Apportioning of Ceramic Assemblages. Journal of Archaeological Science, 39(5): 1513-20. doi: 10.1016/j.jas.2011.12.022 .

See also

Other chronological analysis: aoristic, fit

Author

N. Frerebeau
